category_title: Elementary student with math learning disabilities who relies on semiconcrete reasoning
minimal_context: The student is playing a turn-taking game with Unifix cubes and is asked to find the total number of cubes in 7 towers that have 3 cubes each.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is your turn for 'Please go and bring for me'. Please go and bring for me 7 towers with 3 cubes in each. What is the total number of cubes?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Um, um, I counted to, um, like, I did this, um, 1, 2, 3, then I had it (raises other thumb) then I counted 1, 2, 3...

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: ...and um, when I did it the first time I got messed up... oh my gosh.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: ...so I went to the beginning 1, 2, 3, and 1, I kept it in my head, then 1, 2, 3, that was the second time, then 1, 2, 3, and that was the third time.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is your turn for 'Please go and bring for me'. Please go and bring for me 7 towers with 3 cubes in each. What is the total number of cubes?

STUDENT: Um, um, I counted to, um, like, I did this, um, 1, 2, 3, then I had it (raises other thumb) then I counted 1, 2, 3...

TUTOR: Keep going.

STUDENT: ...and um, when I did it the first time I got messed up... oh my gosh.

TUTOR: What will you do now?

STUDENT: ...so I went to the beginning 1, 2, 3, and 1, I kept it in my head, then 1, 2, 3, that was the second time, then 1, 2, 3, and that was the third time.

task.content: 7 towers with 3 cubes in each, what is the total number of cubes?

context.environment: turn-taking, platform game called “Please go and bring for me ...” (PGBM)
context.materials: box with Unifix Cubes to produce and bring back a tower made of a given number of cubes

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: when I did it the first time I got messed up
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: went to the beginning 1, 2, 3, and 1, I kept it in my head, then 1, 2, 3, that was the second time, then 1, 2, 3, and that was the third time
related_quotes: Um, um, I counted to, um, like, I did this, um, 1, 2, 3, then I had it (raises other thumb) then I counted 1, 2, 3, and um, when I did it the first time I got messed up so I went to the beginning 1, 2, 3, and 1, I kept it in my head, then 1, 2, 3, that was the second time, then 1, 2, 3, and that was the third time.

behavior_patterns.condition: Solving the problem '2 towers of 7, how many cubes do we have'
behavior_patterns.internal_state: recognized his own error as he recounted his process
related_quotes: Um, I knew there were 7 in there so I counted like 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14 —oh my gosh.
